commit.message:
fix: prevent content overflow and left-side text clipping on mobile

Root cause: elements wider than 375px viewport caused content to
shift right, clipping text on the left edge.

Fixes:
- Add overflow-wrapper div in Layout for bulletproof containment
- Replace Tailwind overflow-hidden with inline style overflow:clip
  on hero, features, security sections (more reliable cross-browser)
- Wrap glow orbs in their own overflow:clip container
- Add overflow:clip to terminal parent (shimmer-border extends 1px)
- Constrain hero badge with max-w-full + truncate
- Reduce h1 to text-[1.65rem] on mobile to fit 375px viewport
- Use overflow-wrap:anywhere on h1 for safe word breaking
- Remove max-width:100vw from body (100vw can exceed visual viewport
  on mobile Safari), use width:100% instead

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix orbital jitter: convert server ticks via Hz instead of rate dead-reckoning

Previous approach measured server tick rate in sim-sec/ms and dead-reckoned
between polls. Any Hz variance (Python asyncio isn't perfectly constant)
produced a residual snap every poll. At 10,000x even 2% variance = visible.

New approach: divide server total_ticks by _server_hz (~60, measured from
consecutive polls) to convert to game-seconds. advance(dt) uses delta*speed.
Both paths advance at speed game-sec/real-sec by definition — snaps are
mathematically zero when Hz is accurate, and tiny even when slightly off.

LOCAL mode unchanged (total_ticks is already in game-seconds, divide by
BackendManager mode check in sync_to_ticks).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: preserve certified status after cleanup with proof cache (#203)

Add a `certified_cache` (HashMap<String, CertifiedCacheEntry>) to
CertifiedApi that stores proof information for keys that achieve
Certified status. When a write transitions to Certified (via
process_certifications, process_certifications_with_timeout, or
certified_write's already-certified path), its proof is cached.

get_certified() and get_certification_status() now fall back to
the certified cache when pending_writes has no entry for the key,
ensuring that certified reads remain stable after cleanup_completed,
cleanup_expired, cleanup, and retention eviction.

feat(codegen): tuple values, destructuring bind, while/for guards

commit.message:
Fix tournament timers crash: remove custom queryKeys that broke queryFn

Custom queryKey overrides in useTournamentTimers caused openapi-react-query's
queryFn to destructure wrong values, resulting in "fn is not a function" error.
Also fix error rendering by using error.message instead of the Error object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: redact API keys from log output (#264)

The httpx HTTP client logs full Gemini API URLs at INFO level, exposing
the `key=` query parameter in Modal logs. Add a regex-based redaction
in _InterceptHandler.emit() that replaces `key=<value>` with
`key=[REDACTED]` before forwarding to loguru.

Closes #263

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix profile save: remove extra email field and increase body limit

1. Mobile OnboardingScreen was sending 'email' in the profile payload,
   but the Zod schema uses .strict() which rejects unknown fields.
   Removed email - the server gets it from the JWT token.

2. Increased JSON body limit from 5mb to 20mb to support base64
   photo uploads (6 gallery images can exceed 5mb).

feat: add request logging and audit (Milestone 4)

- migration 005: request_logs table (key_id, model, endpoint,
  prompt_tokens, completion_tokens, latency_ms, status_code,
  truncated_prompt, created_at) with four indexes

- request_log.rs: non-blocking RequestLogger — hot path calls
  try_send() into an mpsc channel (8192 cap); background task
  inserts each LogEntry into request_logs; entry dropped (not
  queued) if channel is full to avoid back-pressure on proxy

- proxy.rs: log every completed request from sync_proxy,
  stream_proxy (at end of background task), and proxy_post_simple;
  key_id passed from proxy_request to avoid redundant DashMap lookup;
  truncated_prompt extracted before body is moved into spawn

- admin.rs: GET /admin/logs — dynamic QueryBuilder query with
  optional ?key_id=, ?model=, ?date=, ?limit= (default 100, max
  1000), ?offset= filters; returns {object,data,limit,offset} JSON

- Grafana: postgres.yml datasource provisioning + request_logs.json
  dashboard (7 panels: request rate, token throughput, P95 latency,
  error rate, tokens by model bar chart, top keys table, recent
  requests table with status-code colour thresholds)

commit.message:
fix: implement causal context for OrSet remove propagation (#200)

OrSet.remove previously only dropped local dots without recording them
as tombstones. This meant merge (a pure union) would resurrect removed
elements when merging with any replica that still held the old dots.

Add a `deferred` (causal context / tombstone) HashSet<Dot> to OrSet,
following the same pattern already used by OrMap:

- remove(): moves element dots into the deferred set
- merge(): only accepts remote dots NOT in our deferred set, and
  removes local dots that ARE in the remote's deferred set
- deferred sets are merged as a union

This preserves add-wins semantics: a concurrent add with a fresh dot
(not in any deferred set) still survives merge.

Serialization includes `#[serde(default)]` on the deferred field for
backward compatibility with data serialized before this change.

commit.message:
fix(frontend): improve WebSocket resilience during long operations

- Increase heartbeat pong timeout from 10s to 25s to tolerate server
  load during heavy Puppeteer operations (prevents premature close)

- Add safety polling (every 15s) in waitForJobViaWebSocket that checks
  job status via HTTP even when WebSocket appears active. This catches
  JOB_COMPLETED events lost during WebSocket disconnection/reconnection
  (code 1006), preventing false timeout errors.

commit.message:
docs(trading-bot): update strategy from $100 to $500 minimum, add trailing grid

Major parameter changes:
- Account: $100 → $500, buying power $500 → $2,500
- Levels: 3 per side → 5 per side (10 total)
- Position size: 0.001 BTC → 0.002 BTC
- Grid range: $1,200 → $2,000
- Net profit per round trip: $0.19 → $0.37

Strategy changes:
- Replace full rebalance with trailing grid (shift one level at a
  time, keep old positions alive, let TPs fill on bounces)
- Emergency trail threshold: 3 levels → 5 levels
- Updated EMA bias: 5/3 levels instead of 3/2
- Updated all examples to use $68,000 BTC price

Updated all three docs files with consistent parameters.

commit.message:
Improve parser syntax error messages with human-readable tokens and help text

- Add Display impl for TokenKind so errors show `(` instead of LParen
- Add error_with_help() to Parser, wiring up the existing Diagnostic help field
- Add contextual errors with syntax templates for all declaration parsers
  (function, derive, mechanic, action, reaction, hook, event, condition,
  move, prompt, table)
- Detect nested system blocks with a clear error instead of confusing
  "expected }, found system"
- In snippet mode (-s), enhance nested-system errors with help explaining
  the auto-wrapping
- Silently skip stray } at top level after prior errors to reduce cascading noise
